acceptance_targets: [chapter_01_F005] PASS only if revised dialogue between snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_01.md:253 and snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_01.md:305 shows at least 2 interruption/overlap beats and each major speaker (Lena, Clay, Cora, Beau) uses contractions in spontaneous lines unless a line is explicitly formal performance. FAIL if the exchange still reads as memo prose.

[chapter_01_F006] PASS only if revised lines between snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_01.md:253 and snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_01.md:283 can be attributed by register without dialogue tags in at least 4 consecutive turns; Clay should foreground institutional control terms, Cora media handling terms, and Lena evidence-chain terms plus clipped pressure lines. FAIL if voices remain interchangeable.

findings.acceptance_test: PASS only if revised dialogue between snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_01.md:253 and snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_01.md:305 shows at least 2 interruption/overlap beats and each major speaker (Lena, Clay, Cora, Beau) uses contractions in spontaneous lines unless a line is explicitly formal performance. FAIL if the exchange still reads as memo prose.
findings.problem: High-pressure argument drifts into over-formal, uncontracted policy cadence, especially in Lena/Clay turns, weakening spoken realism against the style-bible default of high contraction use.
findings.rewrite_direction: Revise briefing exchange between snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_01.md:253 and snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_01.md:305 to preserve institutional conflict while increasing natural spoken rhythm: contractions in non-ceremonial lines, interruptions, and pressure-driven fragments.

findings.acceptance_test: PASS only if revised lines between snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_01.md:253 and snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_01.md:283 can be attributed by register without dialogue tags in at least 4 consecutive turns; Clay should foreground institutional control terms, Cora media handling terms, and Lena evidence-chain terms plus clipped pressure lines. FAIL if voices remain interchangeable.
findings.problem: Idiolect separation softens in the briefing room: Lena, Clay, and Cora often share similar institutional syntax and abstract noun stacks, reducing voice distinctness under pressure.
findings.rewrite_direction: Differentiate voices between snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_01.md:253 and snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_01.md:283: keep Clay in control/optics framing, Cora in media-risk shorthand, and Lena in evidence-procedure logic that snaps into short declaratives when she commits.

non_negotiables: Maintain heading contract exactly (# Chapter N).
Elliptical clipping is not permitted in dialogue or narration.
Avoid transcript cadence in high-stakes dialogue.
Avoid stiff over-formality in dialogue; use contractions and colloquial rhythm where character-true.
Reduce procedural drift when it displaces embodied consequence.
Maintain character-specific voice signatures from style bible.
Honor aesthetic risk policy: sanitization is the primary craft risk — do not soften, euphemize, or retreat from the story's established register. When in doubt, err toward rawness.

revision_pass.focus: Focus on dialogue realism and voice separation: idiolect fidelity, leverage shifts, pressure cadence, anti-transcript behavior, natural colloquial texture (contractions/interruptions/slang when character-true), and pressure-true profanity usage per aesthetic risk policy.
revision_pass.label: dialogue/idiolect/cadence fixes

aesthetic_risk_policy.creative_risk_policy: When edge cases arise, choose the version that preserves uncomfortable specificity and character accountability; do not replace difficult scenes with procedural summary, and do not mute class, race, sexuality, or institutional cruelty when those forces are actively shaping choices.
aesthetic_risk_policy.euphemism_penalty: If a scene with violence, racism, grief, or moral compromise is rendered in abstract or polite shorthand, revise to restore specific bodily, social, and material consequence before accepting the draft.

dialogue_rules.default_contraction_use: high - contractions are the baseline in contemporary Mississippi speech; uncontracted forms are reserved for formality, emphasis, or social performance.

prose_style_profile.chapter_texture_variance: rotate structural textures across chapters: procedural surge, quiet domestic interval, document-driven slow burn, dread preparation, aftermath inventory, and information-turn chapters; avoid repeating scene count plus dialogue density pattern in adjacent chapters.
prose_style_profile.diction: literary but grounded; precise legal and forensic terms when needed, balanced by lived vernacular and local material detail.
prose_style_profile.narrative_distance: close third person anchored primarily to Lena, with brief controlled expansions only when factual clarity requires it.
prose_style_profile.rhythm_target: alternate compressed procedural sentences with longer sensory-emotional lines so pressure and reflection keep rebalancing.
